{"id":"cd797212-65b3-4590-ba49-6bb3e6346bf5","identifier":"FRG-209","title":"Create comprehensive QA test plan from M1 user stories","description":"Create a comprehensive QA test plan for AI Aquarium M1 based on the milestone definition of done. Test against the debug page (not main page — that is M2).

Test areas:

1. AI decisions fire on schedule (30s cycle) — verify via event log
2. All action types work: move, interact, idle, talk
3. Object state transitions: idle→active→idle with correct timers
4. Characters stay at objects for activity duration (stove 30s, bed duration, etc)
5. Character-to-character conversation happens (talk action)
6. Pathfinding respects walkable grid — no movement through walls
7. Debug page shows: character states, object states, decision log, all in real-time

Acceptance:

* Test plan document committed to ai-aquarium-qa repo
* Tests are automated with Playwright where possible
* Manual verification checklist for behaviors that need observation","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-209/create-comprehensive-qa-test-plan-from-m1-user-stories","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-209-create-comprehensive-qa-test-plan-from-m1-user-stories","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:17.473Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:40:55.614Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

     1→// @ts-check
     2→const { defineConfig, devices } = require('@playwright/test');
     3→require('dotenv').config();
     4→
     5→const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app';
     6→
     7→module.exports = defineConfig({
     8→  testDir: './tests',
     9→  timeout: 60_000,
    10→  expect: { timeout: 15_000 },
    11→  fullyParallel: false,
    12→  retries: 1,
    13→  reporter: [['list'], ['json', { outputFile: 'test-results/results.json' }]],
    14→  use: {
    15→    baseURL: BASE_URL,
    16→    headless: true,
    17→    viewport: { width: 1280, height: 960 },

    28→        },
    29→      },
    30→    },
    31→  ],
    32→  outputDir: 'test-results/',
    33→});

USERMESSAGE #17
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.
     4→ * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.
     9→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    10→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    11→ */
    12→async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    13→  await page.waitForFunction(
    14→    () => window._gameEngine &&
    15→          window._gameEngine.characters &&
    16→          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,
    17→    { timeout }
    18→  );
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Read the live engine state.
    23→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    24→ */
    25→async function getEngineState(page) {
    26→  return page.evaluate(() => {
    27→    const eng = window._gameEngine;
    28→    return {
    29→      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),
    30→      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,
    31→      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({
    32→        name: c.name,
    33→        label: c.label,
    34→        tx: c.tx,
    35→        ty: c.ty,
    36→        action: c.action,
    37→        mood: c.mood,
    38→        energy: c.energy,
    39→      })),
    40→      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({
    41→        id: o.id,
    42→        label: o.label,
    43→        state: o.state,
    44→      })),
    45→    };
    46→  });
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.
    51→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    52→ * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate
    53→ * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]
    54→ * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]
    55→ */
    56→async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {
    57→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    58→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    59→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    if (predicate(state)) return state;
    61→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    62→  }
    63→  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    64→}
    65→
    66→module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };
    67→

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_014oYFM8tfvmag2nXGTEzgu3",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.\n     4\u2192 * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.\n     5\u2192 */\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192/**\n     8\u2192 * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.\n     9\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    10\u2192 * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n    11\u2192 */\n    12\u2192async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    13\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    14\u2192    () => window._gameEngine &&\n    15\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters &&\n    16\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    17\u2192    { timeout }\n    18\u2192  );\n    19\u2192}\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * Read the live engine state.\n    23\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192async function getEngineState(page) {\n    26\u2192  return page.evaluate(() => {\n    27\u2192    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    28\u2192    return {\n    29\u2192      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),\n    30\u2192      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,\n    31\u2192      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({\n    32\u2192        name: c.name,\n    33\u2192        label: c.label,\n    34\u2192        tx: c.tx,\n    35\u2192        ty: c.ty,\n    36\u2192        action: c.action,\n    37\u2192        mood: c.mood,\n    38\u2192        energy: c.energy,\n    39\u2192      })),\n    40\u2192      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({\n    41\u2192        id: o.id,\n    42\u2192        label: o.label,\n    43\u2192        state: o.state,\n    44\u2192      })),\n    45\u2192    };\n    46\u2192  });\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.\n    51\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    52\u2192 * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate\n    53\u2192 * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]\n    54\u2192 * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]\n    55\u2192 */\n    56\u2192async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {\n    57\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n    58\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    59\u2192    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    60\u2192    if (predicate(state)) return state;\n    61\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };\n    67\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #19
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * SMOKE — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies the sim page loads, Phaser boots, and all 3 characters are present.
     5→ */
     6→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     7→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     8→
     9→test.describe('Smoke: App loads and sim initialises', () => {
    10→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    11→    await page.goto('/');
    12→    await waitForEngine(page);
    13→  });
    14→
    15→  test('page title is correct', async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  test('Phaser canvas renders', async ({ page }) => {
    20→    const canvas = page.locator('canvas');
    21→    await expect(canvas).toBeVisible();
    22→  });
    23→
    24→  test('sim clock is visible and ticking', async ({ page }) => {
    25→    // Clock text is rendered by Phaser into the canvas — read via engine
    26→    const state1 = await getEngineState(page);
    27→    expect(state1.simTime).toMatch(/^\d+:\d{2} (AM|PM)$/);
    28→
    29→    // Verify via raw simTimeMs (more precise than string comparison)
    30→    // simSpeed = 60 → 1 real second = 60 sim seconds
    31→    const ms0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    32→    // Wait 5 s → expect ~300 sim seconds (5 real min) of advancement
    33→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
    34→    const ms1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    35→
    36→    expect(ms1, 'simTimeMs did not advance — Phaser update loop may not be running').toBeGreaterThan(ms0);
    37→  });
    38→
    39→  test('exactly 3 characters are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    40→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    expect(state.characters).toHaveLength(3);
    42→  });
    43→
    44→  test('characters have expected names', async ({ page }) => {
    45→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    46→    const labels = state.characters.map(c => c.label).sort();
    47→    expect(labels).toEqual(['Maple', 'Nyx', 'Sol']);
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  test('AI engine is enabled', async ({ page }) => {
    51→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    52→    expect(state.aiEnabled).toBe(true);
    53→  });
    54→
    55→  test('all 10 interactive objects are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    56→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    57→    expect(state.objectStates).toHaveLength(10);
    58→    const ids = state.objectStates.map(o => o.id).sort();
    59→    expect(ids).toEqual([
    60→      'bed', 'bonsai', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel',
    61→      'fridge', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'tea_set', 'tv',
    62→    ]);
    63→  });
    64→
    65→  test('debug panel opens and shows object states', async ({ page }) => {
    66→    const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });
    67→    await expect(debugBtn).toBeVisible();
    68→    await debugBtn.click();
    69→    await expect(page.getByText('OBJECT STATES')).toBeVisible();
    70→    await expect(page.getByText('Stove')).toBeVisible();
    71→  });
    72→});
    73→

USERMESSAGE #21
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that each character queries the LLM API and receives valid decisions.
     5→ */
     6→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     7→const { waitForEngine } = require('../helpers');
     8→
     9→test.describe('Regression: AI decision-making via /api/llm', () => {
    10→  test('each character sends a POST to /api/llm on page load', async ({ page }) => {
    11→    const llmRequests = [];
    12→
    13→    page.on('request', req => {
    14→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    15→        llmRequests.push(req);
    16→      }
    17→    });
    18→
    19→    await page.goto('/');
    20→    await waitForEngine(page);
    21→
    22→    // All 3 characters fire immediately (timer starts at 0)
    23→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000); // allow time for all 3 requests
    24→
    25→    expect(llmRequests.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    26→  });
    27→
    28→  test('LLM request body includes character name, position, and nearby objects', async ({ page }) => {
    29→    const bodies = [];
    30→
    31→    page.on('request', req => {
    32→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    33→        try {
    34→          bodies.push(JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'));
    35→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
    36→      }
    37→    });
    38→
    39→    await page.goto('/');
    40→    await waitForEngine(page);
    41→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
    42→
    43→    expect(bodies.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    44→
    45→    for (const body of bodies) {
    46→      // System prompt includes a character name
    47→      expect(body.system).toMatch(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
    48→      // Includes mood and energy
    49→      expect(body.system).toMatch(/mood.*\/100.*energy.*\/100/);
    50→      // User message includes sim time, tile position, nearby objects
    51→      const userContent = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
    52→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/Sim time:/);
    53→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/You are at tile/);
    54→      expect(userContent).toMatch(/Nearby objects:/);
    55→      // max_tokens set to 256
    56→      expect(body.max_tokens).toBe(256);
    57→    }
    58→  });
    59→
    60→  test('LLM responses are valid JSON with action field', async ({ page }) => {
    61→    const responses = [];
    62→
    63→    page.on('response', async res => {
    64→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
    65→        try {
    66→          const json = await res.json();
    67→          // Extract content from API response (Anthropic/OpenAI shape)
    68→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text
    69→                    ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    70→          if (text) responses.push(JSON.parse(text));
    71→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
    72→      }
    73→    });
    74→
    75→    await page.goto('/');
    76→    await waitForEngine(page);
    77→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
    78→
    79→    expect(responses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    80→
    81→    for (const decision of responses) {
    82→      expect(decision).toHaveProperty('action');
    83→      expect(['move', 'interact', 'idle']).toContain(decision.action);
    84→
    85→      if (decision.action === 'move') {
    86→        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetTile');
    87→        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.tx).toBe('number');
    88→        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.ty).toBe('number');
    89→      }
    90→      if (decision.action === 'interact') {
    91→        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetId');
    92→        expect(typeof decision.targetId).toBe('string');
    93→      }
    94→    }
    95→  });
    96→
    97→  test('each character only receives one decision per 30s interval', async ({ page }) => {
    98→    const byChar = new Map();
    99→
   100→    page.on('request', req => {
   101→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
   102→        try {
   103→          const body = JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}');
   104→          const match = (body.system || '').match(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
   105→          if (match) {
   106→            const name = match[1];
   107→            byChar.set(name, (byChar.get(name) || 0) + 1);
   108→          }
   109→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   110→      }
   111→    });
   112→
   113→    await page.goto('/');
   114→    await waitForEngine(page);
   115→
   116→    // Wait well under one interval (< 30 s) — each char should have exactly 1 request
   117→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   118→
   119→    expect(byChar.size).toBe(3); // All 3 characters sent a request
   120→    for (const [name, count] of byChar) {
   121→      expect(count, `${name} sent ${count} requests (expected 1 in first 10 s)`).toBe(1);
   122→    }
   123→  });
   124→
   125→  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {
   126→    const statuses = [];
   127→
   128→    page.on('response', res => {
   129→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
   130→        statuses.push(res.status());
   131→      }
   132→    });
   133→
   134→    await page.goto('/');
   135→    await waitForEngine(page);
   136→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
   137→
   138→    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
   139→    for (const status of statuses) {
   140→      expect(status).toBe(200);
   141→    }
   142→  });
   143→});
   144→

80\u2192\n    81\u2192    for (const decision of responses) {\n    82\u2192      expect(decision).toHaveProperty('action');\n    83\u2192      expect(['move', 'interact', 'idle']).toContain(decision.action);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192      if (decision.action === 'move') {\n    86\u2192        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetTile');\n    87\u2192        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.tx).toBe('number');\n    88\u2192        expect(typeof decision.targetTile.ty).toBe('number');\n    89\u2192      }\n    90\u2192      if (decision.action === 'interact') {\n    91\u2192        expect(decision).toHaveProperty('targetId');\n    92\u2192        expect(typeof decision.targetId).toBe('string');\n    93\u2192      }\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192  });\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  test('each character only receives one decision per 30s interval', async ({ page }) => {\n    98\u2192    const byChar = new Map();\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n   101\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n   102\u2192        try {\n   103\u2192          const body = JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}');\n   104\u2192          const match = (body.system || '').match(/You are (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n   105\u2192          if (match) {\n   106\u2192            const name = match[1];\n   107\u2192            byChar.set(name, (byChar.get(name) || 0) + 1);\n   108\u2192          }\n   109\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   110\u2192      }\n   111\u2192    });\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   114\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    // Wait well under one interval (< 30 s) \u2014 each char should have exactly 1 request\n   117\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    expect(byChar.size).toBe(3); // All 3 characters sent a request\n   120\u2192    for (const [name, count] of byChar) {\n   121\u2192      expect(count, `${name} sent ${count} requests (expected 1 in first 10 s)`).toBe(1);\n   122\u2192    }\n   123\u2192  });\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {\n   126\u2192    const statuses = [];\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    page.on('response', res => {\n   129\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n   130\u2192        statuses.push(res.status());\n   131\u2192      }\n   132\u2192    });\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   135\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   136\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n   139\u2192    for (const status of statuses) {\n   140\u2192      expect(status).toBe(200);\n   141\u2192    }\n   142\u2192  });\n   143\u2192});\n   144\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }

USERMESSAGE #23
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that characters move via A* pathfinding (action = 'walking',
     5→ * tile coordinates change over time).
     6→ */
     7→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     8→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     9→
    10→test.describe('Regression: Character movement via A*', () => {
    11→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    12→    await page.goto('/');
    13→    await waitForEngine(page);
    14→  });
    15→
    16→  test('at least one character enters walking state within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    17→    const finalState = await pollEngineState(
    18→      page,
    19→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    20→      15_000
    21→    );
    22→    const walking = finalState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'walking');
    23→    expect(walking.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  test('walking character tile position changes over time', async ({ page }) => {
    27→    // Wait until someone walks
    28→    const state1 = await pollEngineState(
    29→      page,
    30→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    31→      15_000
    32→    );
    33→
    34→    const walker = state1.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking');
    35→    const startTx = walker.tx;
    36→    const startTy = walker.ty;
    37→
    38→    // Wait for movement to complete
    39→    await page.waitForTimeout(6000);
    40→    const state2 = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    const walkerAfter = state2.characters.find(c => c.name === walker.name);
    42→
    43→    // Position must have changed
    44→    const moved = walkerAfter.tx !== startTx || walkerAfter.ty !== startTy;
    45→    expect(moved, `${walker.label} did not move from (${startTx}, ${startTy})`).toBe(true);
    46→  });
    47→
    48→  test('characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1–17, rows 2–9)', async ({ page }) => {
    49→    // Sample character positions over 10 seconds
    50→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    51→      await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    52→      const state = await getEngineState(page);
    53→      for (const c of state.characters) {
    54→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    55→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);
    56→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
    57→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);
    58→      }
    59→    }
    60→  });
    61→
    62→  test('character action transitions: idle → walking → idle', async ({ page }) => {
    63→    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle
    64→    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(
    65→      page,
    66→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    67→      15_000
    68→    );
    69→    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;
    70→
    71→    // Wait for that character to return to idle
    72→    const idleState = await pollEngineState(
    73→      page,
    74→      state => state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName)?.action === 'idle',
    75→      20_000
    76→    );
    77→    const charIdle = idleState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);
    78→    expect(charIdle.action).toBe('idle');
    79→  });
    80→});
    81→

of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n    57\u2192        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n    58\u2192      }\n    59\u2192    }\n    60\u2192  });\n const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(\n    65\u2192      page,\n    66\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n    67\u2192      15_000\n    68\u2192    );\n    69\u2192    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    // Wait for that character to return to idle\n    72\u2192    const ▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {

    "id": "toolu_01QppAPSiXBYsXT1vr6ZmQ14",

USERMESSAGE #25
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that object state changes are visible and that interactions
     5→ * (both random-wander and AI-triggered) work correctly.
     6→ *
     7→ * Also captures the known bug: AIEngine uses `_pendingInteract` but
     8→ * SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks `targetObjectId`, so
     9→ * AI-driven interactions never fire object state transitions.
    10→ */
    11→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    12→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    13→
    14→test.describe('Regression: Object interaction and state changes', () => {
    15→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await page.goto('/');
    17→    await waitForEngine(page);
    18→  });
    19→
    20→  test('at least one object changes state within 60 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    21→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    22→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    23→
    24→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    25→      page,
    26→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    27→      60_000,
    28→      1000
    29→    );
    30→
    31→    const changedObjects = changed.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    32→    expect(changedObjects.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    33→    console.log('Objects that changed state:', changedObjects.map(o => `${o.id}: ${initialMap[o.id]} → ${o.state}`).join(', '));
    34→  });
    35→
    36→  test('debug panel reflects live object state changes', async ({ page }) => {
    37→    // Open debug panel
    38→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    39→
    40→    // Wait for an object to go non-idle
    41→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    42→      page,
    43→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle'),
    44→      60_000,
    45→      1000
    46→    );
    47→
    48→    const nonIdle = changed.objectStates.find(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    49→    expect(nonIdle).toBeTruthy();
    50→
    51→    // The debug panel should reflect the new state
    52→    const stateText = page.getByText(nonIdle.state, { exact: false });
    53→    await expect(stateText).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });
    54→  });
    55→
    56→  test('debug panel "use" button triggers object state change', async ({ page }) => {
    57→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    58→
    59→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    const idleObject = initial.objectStates.find(o => o.state === 'idle');
    61→    expect(idleObject, 'Need at least one idle object').toBeTruthy();
    62→
    63→    // Find and click the "use" button for that object
    64→    // The debug panel rows are: ObjectName | stateLabel | useButton
    65→    const objectLabel = idleObject.label;
    66→    const row = page.locator(`text=${objectLabel}`).locator('..').first();
    67→    const useBtn = row.getByRole('button', { name: /use/i });
    68→
    69→    // Fall back: just find any use button
    70→    const anyUseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' }).first();
    71→    await anyUseBtn.click();
    72→
    73→    // State should change
    74→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    75→    const after = await getEngineState(page);
    76→    const anyNonIdle = after.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    77→    expect(anyNonIdle, 'No object changed state after clicking "use"').toBe(true);
    78→  });
    79→
    80→  test('object auto-transitions back to idle after timed duration', async ({ page }) => {
    81→    // Target the bonsai specifically: auto_next = 12 real seconds (fastest cycle)
    82→    // Test timeout extended to allow the full cycle
    83→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    84→
    85→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    86→
    87→    // Directly trigger bonsai via engine (avoids DOM layout uncertainty)
    88→    await page.evaluate(() => {
    89→      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');
    90→    });
    91→
    92→    // Confirm bonsai is now in 'tending'
    93→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    94→    const activated = await getEngineState(page);
    95→    const bonsai = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
    96→    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be in tending state').toBe('tending');
    97→
    98→    console.log('Bonsai transitioned to tending, waiting for auto-return to idle (12s)...');
    99→
   100→    // Wait for bonsai auto_next: idle after 12 real seconds
   101→    const returned = await pollEngineState(
   102→      page,
   103→      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',
   104→      30_000,
   105→      500
   106→    );
   107→    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
   108→    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');
   109→  });
   110→
   111→  // ── BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {
   113→    // This test documents the known bug:
   114→    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id
   115→    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)
   116→    // Result: AI-driven "interact" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.
   117→
   118→    const interactDecisions = [];
   119→    page.on('response', async res => {
   120→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
   121→        try {
   122→          const json = await res.json();
   123→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   124→          if (text) {
   125→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   126→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);
   127→          }
   128→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   129→      }
   130→    });
   131→
   132→    await page.goto('/');
   133→    await waitForEngine(page);
   134→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions
   135→
   136→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   137→      // No interact decisions in this run — test is inconclusive, skip gracefully
   138→      console.log('[BUG test] No "interact" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');
   139→      return;
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    // Verify: after an AI "interact" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set
   143→    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)
   144→    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {
   145→      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
   146→      return chars.map(c => ({
   147→        name: c.name,
   148→        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,
   149→        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,
   150→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,
   151→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,
   152→      }));
   153→    });
   154→
   155→    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));
   156→
   157→    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId
   158→    // This confirms the mismatch
   159→    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);
   160→    if (bugged.length > 0) {
   161→      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null — AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) — the regression
   165→    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.
   166→    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions
   167→  });
   168→});
   169→

changedObjects.map(o => `${o.id}: ${initialMap[o.id]} \u2192 ${o.state}`).join(', '));\n    34\u2192  });\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  test('debug panel reflects live object state changes', async ({ page }) => {\n    37\u2192    // Open debug panel\n    38\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    // Wait for an object to go non-idle\n    41\u2192    const changed = await pollEngineState(\n    42\u2192      page,\n    43\u2192      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle'),\n    44\u2192      60_000,\n    45\u2192      1000\n    46\u2192    );\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192    const nonIdle = changed.objectStates.find(o => o.state !== 'idle');\n    49\u2192    expect(nonIdle).toBeTruthy();\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    // The debug panel should reflect the new state\n    52\u2192    const stateText = page.getByText(nonIdle.state, { exact: false });\n    53\u2192    await expect(stateText).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n    54\u2192  });\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192  test('debug panel \"use\" button triggers object state change', async ({ page }) => {\n    57\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    const initial = await getEngineState(page);\n    60\u2192    const idleObject = initial.objectStates.find(o => o.state === 'idle');\n    61\u2192    expect(idleObject, 'Need at least one idle object').toBeTruthy();\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    // Find and click the \"use\" button for that object\n    64\u2192    // The debug panel rows are: ObjectName | stateLabel | useButton\n    65\u2192    const objectLabel = idleObject.label;\n    66\u2192    const row = page.locator(`text=${objectLabel}`).locator('..').first();\n    67\u2192    const useBtn = row.getByRole('button', { name: /use/i });\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    // Fall back: just find any use button\n    70\u2192    const anyUseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' }).first();\n    71\u2192    await anyUseBtn.click();\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    // State should change\n    74\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    75\u2192    const after = await getEngineState(page);\n    76\u2192    const anyNonIdle = after.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle');\n    77\u2192    expect(anyNonIdle, 'No object changed state after clicking \"use\"').toBe(true);\n    78\u2192  });\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  test('object auto-transitions back to idle after timed duration', async ({ page }) => {\n    81\u2192    // Target the bonsai specifically: auto_next = 12 real seconds (fastest cycle)\n    82\u2192    // Test timeout extended to allow the full cycle\n    83\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    // Directly trigger bonsai via engine (avoids DOM layout uncertainty)\n    88\u2192    await page.evaluate(() => {\n    89\u2192      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');\n    90\u2192    });\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    // Confirm bonsai is now in 'tending'\n    93\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    94\u2192    const activated = await getEngineState(page);\n    95\u2192    const bonsai = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n    96\u2192    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be in tending state').toBe('tending');\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    console.log('Bonsai transitioned to tending, waiting for auto-return to idle (12s)...');\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    // Wait for bonsai auto_next: idle after 12 real seconds\n   101\u2192    const returned = await pollEngineState(\n   102\u2192      page,\n   103\u2192      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',\n   104\u2192      30_000,\n   105\u2192      500\n   106\u2192    );\n   107\u2192    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n   108\u2192    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');\n   109\u2192  });\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   112\u2192  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {\n   113\u2192    // This test documents the known bug:\n   114\u2192    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id\n   115\u2192    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)\n   116\u2192    // Result: AI-driven \"interact\" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    const interactDecisions = [];\n   119\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   120\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n   121\u2192        try {\n   122\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   123\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   124\u2192          if (text) {\n   125\u2192            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n   126\u2192            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);\n   127\u2192          }\n   128\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192    });\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   133\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   134\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {\n   137\u2192      // No interact decisions in this run \u2014 test is inconclusive, skip gracefully\n   138\u2192      console.log('[BUG test] No \"interact\" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');\n   139\u2192      return;\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    // Verify: after an AI \"interact\" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set\n   143\u2192    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)\n   144\u2192    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   145\u2192      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];\n   146\u2192      return chars.map(c => ({\n   147\u2192        name: c.name,\n   148\u2192        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,\n   149\u2192        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,\n   150\u2192        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,\n   151\u2192        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n   152\u2192      }));\n   153\u2192    });\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId\n   158\u2192    // This confirms the mismatch\n   159\u2192    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);\n   160\u2192    if (bugged.length > 0) {\n   161\u2192      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null \u2014 AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) \u2014 the regression\n   165\u2192    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.\n   166\u2192    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions\n   167\u2192  });\n   168\u2192});\n   169\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that speech bubbles appear (characters talk via pendingSpeech).
     5→ * Speech bubbles are Phaser canvas objects, so we verify via:
     6→ *  1. Console log interception (AIEngine logs descriptions)
     7→ *  2. Engine state: pendingSpeech is set and cleared
     8→ *  3. DOM: the canvas updates (indirect)
     9→ */
    10→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    11→const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    12→
    13→test.describe('Regression: Speech bubbles and character dialogue', () => {
    14→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    15→    await page.goto('/');
    16→    await waitForEngine(page);
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  test('AI engine logs character descriptions to console', async ({ page }) => {
    20→    const descriptions = [];
    21→
    22→    page.on('console', msg => {
    23→      if (msg.type() === 'log') {
    24→        const text = msg.text();
    25→        if (text.match(/^\[(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\]/)) {
    26→          descriptions.push(text);
    27→        }
    28→      }
    29→    });
    30→
    31→    await page.goto('/');
    32→    await waitForEngine(page);
    33→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
    34→
    35→    expect(descriptions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    36→    for (const desc of descriptions) {
    37→      expect(desc).toMatch(/^\[(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\]/);
    38→    }
    39→  });
    40→
    41→  test('AI dialogue is stored on character after LLM response', async ({ page }) => {
    42→    // Characters get currentDialogue set from LLM response
    43→    // Poll until at least one character has a non-null currentDialogue
    44→    const hasSpeech = await page.waitForFunction(
    45→      () => {
    46→        const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
    47→        return chars.some(c => c.currentDialogue !== null && c.currentDialogue !== undefined);
    48→      },
    49→      { timeout: 15_000 }
    50→    ).catch(() => null);
    51→
    52→    // currentDialogue may be brief (5s timer), so also check pendingSpeech
    53→    const hasEitherSpeech = await page.evaluate(() => {
    54→      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
    55→      return chars.map(c => ({
    56→        name: c.label,
    57→        currentDialogue: c.currentDialogue,
    58→        pendingSpeech: c.pendingSpeech,
    59→        dialogueLines: c.dialogue,
    60→      }));
    61→    });
    62→
    63→    // Each character should have preset dialogue lines loaded
    64→    for (const c of hasEitherSpeech) {
    65→      expect(c.dialogueLines.length, `${c.name} has no dialogue lines`).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    console.log('Character speech state:', JSON.stringify(hasEitherSpeech, null, 2));
    69→  });
    70→
    71→  test('characters have preset dialogue lines defined', async ({ page }) => {
    72→    const chars = await page.evaluate(() =>
    73→      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
    74→        label: c.label,
    75→        lines: c.dialogue,
    76→      }))
    77→    );
    78→
    79→    const mapleChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Maple');
    80→    const solChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Sol');
    81→    const nyxChar = chars.find(c => c.label === 'Nyx');
    82→
    83→    expect(mapleChar?.lines).toContain('Perfect brew today~');
    84→    expect(solChar?.lines).toContain('The bonsai looks healthy');
    85→    expect(nyxChar?.lines).toContain('I need more paint...');
    86→  });
    87→
    88→  test('speech timer fires and sets pendingSpeech within 45 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    89→    // pendingSpeech is set AND cleared within a single RAF frame, so polling can't catch it.
    90→    // Instead, we patch the engine in-page to intercept pendingSpeech assignment via a setter.
    91→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
    92→
    93→    await page.goto('/');
    94→    await waitForEngine(page);
    95→
    96→    // Patch characters to capture speech via property descriptor trap
    97→    await page.evaluate(() => {
    98→      window._qaSpeechLog = [];
    99→      const chars = window._gameEngine.characters;
   100→      for (const char of chars) {
   101→        let _speech = char.pendingSpeech;
   102→        Object.defineProperty(char, 'pendingSpeech', {
   103→          get() { return _speech; },
   104→          set(val) {
   105→            if (val) window._qaSpeechLog.push({ name: char.label, text: val, ts: Date.now() });
   106→            _speech = val;
   107→          },
   108→          configurable: true,
   109→        });
   110→      }
   111→    });
   112→
   113→    // Now wait for speech to fire (20–40s real time per character)
   114→    await page.waitForFunction(
   115→      () => window._qaSpeechLog?.length > 0,
   116→      { timeout: 50_000, polling: 500 }
   117→    );
   118→
   119→    const log = await page.evaluate(() => window._qaSpeechLog);
   120→    console.log('Speech log:', log);
   121→
   122→    expect(log.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   123→    for (const entry of log) {
   124→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.name);
   125→      expect(typeof entry.text).toBe('string');
   126→      expect(entry.text.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   127→    }
   128→  });
   129→});
   130→

USERMESSAGE #29
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * CRITICAL — FRG-184
     4→ * End-to-end sim loop integrity:
     5→ *   1. LLM decisions fire and are applied
     6→ *   2. Characters move (A*)
     7→ *   3. Object states change (visible in debug panel)
     8→ *   4. Sim clock advances in real-time
     9→ *   5. No console errors from simulation code
    10→ *
    11→ * Also documents the critical AI interact bug (FRG-184).
    12→ */
    13→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    14→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    15→
    16→test.describe('Critical: Full sim loop integrity (FRG-184)', () => {
    17→  test('complete sim cycle: LLM fires → character moves → object interacted', async ({ page }) => {
    18→    const llmFired = [];
    19→    const simErrors = [];
    20→
    21→    page.on('request', req => {
    22→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    23→        llmFired.push(req.url());
    24→      }
    25→    });
    26→
    27→    page.on('console', msg => {
    28→      if (msg.type() === 'error' && !msg.text().includes('favicon')) {
    29→        simErrors.push(msg.text());
    30→      }
    31→      // Catch AI engine warnings about bad responses
    32→      if (msg.type() === 'warn' && msg.text().includes('AIEngine')) {
    33→        simErrors.push(`[AIEngine WARN] ${msg.text()}`);
    34→      }
    35→    });
    36→
    37→    await page.goto('/');
    38→    await waitForEngine(page);
    39→
    40→    // Step 1: LLM decisions fire
    41→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
    42→    expect(llmFired.length, 'Expected at least 3 LLM requests').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    43→
    44→    // Step 2: Characters move
    45→    const walkState = await pollEngineState(
    46→      page,
    47→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    48→      15_000
    49→    );
    50→    expect(walkState.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);
    51→
    52→    // Step 3: An object changes state (from random wander which is always working)
    53→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    54→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    55→
    56→    const changedState = await pollEngineState(
    57→      page,
    58→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    59→      60_000,
    60→      500
    61→    );
    62→    const changed = changedState.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    63→    expect(changed.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    64→
    65→    // Step 4: Sim clock advances
    66→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    67→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    68→    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    69→    expect(t2).toBeGreaterThan(t1);
    70→
    71→    // Step 5: No sim errors
    72→    const criticalErrors = simErrors.filter(e =>
    73→      !e.includes('swiftshader') && !e.includes('WebGL') && !e.includes('ReadPixels')
    74→    );
    75→    if (criticalErrors.length > 0) {
    76→      console.warn('Simulation errors found:', criticalErrors);
    77→    }
    78→    expect(criticalErrors, 'Unexpected console errors in sim').toHaveLength(0);
    79→  });
    80→
    81→  test('[FIXED FRG-184] AI interact decisions no longer set _pendingInteract — targetObjectId pathway is used', async ({ page }) => {
    82→    /**
    83→     * Previously broken: AIEngine._applyDecision (interact case) set character._pendingInteract
    84→     * instead of character.targetObjectId, so SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival never fired.
    85→     *
    86→     * FIXED: _pendingInteract is no longer set by the AI engine. The correct property
    87→     * (targetObjectId) is now used, or the interaction is handled via a different pathway.
    88→     *
    89→     * This test verifies the OLD broken behavior is gone: after an AI "interact" decision,
    90→     * _pendingInteract must NOT be set on any character.
    91→     */
    92→    const interactDecisions = [];
    93→
    94→    page.on('response', async res => {
    95→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
    96→        try {
    97→          const json = await res.json();
    98→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    99→          if (text) {
   100→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   101→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push({ ...d, ts: Date.now() });
   102→          }
   103→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   104→      }
   105→    });
   106→
   107→    await page.goto('/');
   108→    await waitForEngine(page);
   109→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   110→
   111→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   112→      console.log('No AI "interact" decisions in this run — skipping bug verification');
   113→      return;
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // After AI decides to interact, verify targetObjectId is NOT set
   117→    const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>
   118→      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
   119→        label: c.label,
   120→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract ?? null,
   121→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId ?? null,
   122→      }))
   123→    );
   124→
   125→    // Verify the fix: _pendingInteract must NOT be set by the AI engine
   126→    console.log('[FIXED FRG-184] Character state after AI interact decision:');
   127→    console.log(JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));
   128→    console.log('AI interact decisions received:', interactDecisions);
   129→
   130→    for (const char of charState) {
   131→      expect(char.pendingInteract,
   132→        `[REGRESSION] ${char.label}: _pendingInteract is set — old broken code path is back`
   133→      ).toBeNull();
   134→    }
   135→  });
   136→
   137→  test('sim clock advances (simTimeMs increases over real time)', async ({ page }) => {
   138→    // Note: In headless Playwright, RAF is throttled (~25% of real time).
   139→    // We verify the clock advances but do not enforce the exact 60x speed ratio
   140→    // (that ratio holds in a real browser; see live browser validation above).
   141→    await page.goto('/');
   142→    await waitForEngine(page);
   143→
   144→    const t0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   145→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000); // 5 real seconds
   146→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   147→
   148→    const simElapsed = t1 - t0;
   149→
   150→    // Clock must advance (not frozen)
   151→    expect(simElapsed, 'simTimeMs did not advance — game loop may be broken').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   152→
   153→    // In headless, simSpeed=60 but RAF throttles to ~25% real time.
   154→    // Minimum acceptable: >5 sim seconds of advancement per 5 real seconds (1:1 floor).
   155→    expect(simElapsed, 'sim clock advancing too slowly').toBeGreaterThan(5000);
   156→
   157→    const ratio = simElapsed / 5000;
   158→    console.log(`Sim speed ratio: ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x (expected 60x in production, ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x in headless)`);
   159→  });
   160→
   161→  test('character mood and energy are initialised at 100', async ({ page }) => {
   162→    await page.goto('/');
   163→    await waitForEngine(page);
   164→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
   165→    for (const c of state.characters) {
   166→      expect(c.mood, `${c.label} mood`).toBe(100);
   167→      expect(c.energy, `${c.label} energy`).toBe(100);
   168→    }
   169→  });
   170→
   171→  test('no 5xx errors from /api/llm', async ({ page }) => {
   172→    const errors = [];
   173→    page.on('response', res => {
   174→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.status() >= 500) {
   175→        errors.push({ url: res.url(), status: res.status() });
   176→      }
   177→    });
   178→
   179→    await page.goto('/');
   180→    await waitForEngine(page);
   181→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   182→
   183→    expect(errors, 'Server errors from /api/llm').toHaveLength(0);
   184→  });
   185→});
   186→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #30
Now I have a good understanding of the existing test suite. Let me review the FRG-209 issue: FRG-209: "Create comprehensive QA test plan from M1 user stories" The issue requires: 1. AI decisions fire on schedule (30s cycle) — verify via event log 2. All action types work: move, interact, idle, talk 3. Object state transitions: idle→active→idle with correct timers 4. Characters stay at objects for activity duration (stove 30s, bed duration, etc) 5. Character-to-character conversation happens (talk action) 6. Pathfinding respects walkable grid — no movement through walls 7. Debug page shows: character states, object states, decision log, all in real-time Acceptance criteria include a test plan document committed to the repo, automated tests with Playwright where feasible, and a manual verification checklist for observable behaviors. I need to map the existing tests against these M1 criteria, create the test plan document, fill in any gaps with new automated tests, and build out the manual checklist. Let me start by updating the issue status and running the current test suite to see what's already covered. Looking at the test coverage, I can see the AI decision cycle is only partially tested—the existing tests check initial decisions but don't verify the 30-second repeat timing. Move and idle actions are covered, but the talk action between characters isn't explicitly tested. Object state transitions are handled, but characters staying at objects for the full activity duration isn't verified. Pathfinding tests check bounds but not wall avoidance specifically. The debug page gets a smoke test but nothing comprehensive. I'll need to add tests for the 30-second cycle timing, the talk action, activity duration, wall avoidance, and more thorough debug panel verification. Let me start by updating the issue status and running the existing tests to see what's currently passing, then I'll create targeted tests for these gaps and document the test plan.

{"id":"cd797212-65b3-4590-ba49-6bb3e6346bf5","identifier":"FRG-209","title":"Create comprehensive QA test plan from M1 user stories","description":"Create a comprehensive QA test plan for AI Aquarium M1 based on the milestone definition of done. Test against the debug page (not main page — that is M2).

Test areas:

1. AI decisions fire on schedule (30s cycle) — verify via event log
2. All action types work: move, interact, idle, talk
3. Object state transitions: idle→active→idle with correct timers
4. Characters stay at objects for activity duration (stove 30s, bed duration, etc)
5. Character-to-character conversation happens (talk action)
6. Pathfinding respects walkable grid — no movement through walls
7. Debug page shows: character states, object states, decision log, all in real-time

Acceptance:

* Test plan document committed to ai-aquarium-qa repo
* Tests are automated with Playwright where possible
* Manual verification checklist for behaviors that need observation","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-209/create-comprehensive-qa-test-plan-from-m1-user-stories","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-209-create-comprehensive-qa-test-plan-from-m1-user-stories","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:17.473Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:40:55.614Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #43
The regression tests show one failure: "character action transitions: idle → walking → idle" - it timed out waiting for a character to return to idle after walking within 20 seconds. The character also has Amelia, Lucy, and Ash names instead of Maple, Sol, Nyx. Let me note that issue. Also note: characters seem to be named "Amelia", "Lucy", "Ash" in the engine instead of "Maple", "Sol", "Nyx"... wait, no - the engine uses `name` and `label`. The smoke test checks for labels "Maple", "Nyx", "Sol" and that passed. So the BUG test is querying by internal `name` field which returns "Amelia", "Lucy", "Ash", while the smoke test passed because it was checking the display `label` field instead. Now let me run the critical tests.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #46
The existing tests show: - Smoke: 8/8 pass ✅ - Regression: 17/18 pass (1 failure: "character action transitions: idle → walking → idle" - timeout on the idle return state) - Critical: 5/5 pass ✅ Now let me analyze the M1 gaps and write new tests. The FRG-209 issue asks for: 1. AI decisions fire on schedule (30s cycle) — verify via event log 2. All action types work: move, interact, idle, talk 3. Object state transitions: idle→active→idle with correct timers 4. Characters stay at objects for activity duration (stove 30s, bed duration, etc) 5. Character-to-character conversation happens (talk action) 6. Pathfinding respects walkable grid — no movement through walls 7. Debug page shows: character states, object states, decision log, all in real-time Looking at the coverage gaps, the 30s cycle is only partially tested in ai-decisions.spec.js since it just verifies the first round of decisions without checking if the cycle repeats. The talk action type is completely missing from coverage, while move, idle, and interact are already tested. Object state transitions are covered, but characters staying at objects for their activity duration isn't explicitly tested. The debug page has smoke tests for opening and showing object states, but the decision log display isn't verified. I also need to check that pathfinding actually avoids walls, not just bounds. The main gaps I need to fill are verifying the 30s AI cycle repeats by checking request timing, testing the talk action type specifically, and ensuring the decision log appears on the debug page. Let me explore what the debug panel actually displays so I can write targeted tests for character states and the decision log. I need to create a TEST_PLAN.md file as required by the issue. Looking at the failing test about character transitions, the 20-second timeout for returning to idle seems tight—the character needs to finish walking and potentially do an activity first, so this might be a test timing issue rather than a real bug. Now I'm writing the new M1 tests to cover the gaps: verifying the 30-second AI decision cycle fires twice within 40 seconds, checking if the LLM supports a talk action type, and confirming characters stay near objects during activities. I also need to check what the debug panel actually displays—specifically whether it shows character states and AI decision logs—so I'll run a quick script to inspect the debug page.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #48
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/probe-debug.js << 'EOF'\n'use strict';\nconst { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');\nrequire('dotenv').config();\n\n(async () => {\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ args: ['--enable-unsafe-swiftshader', '--disable-gpu'], headless: true });\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.goto(process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app');\n  \n  // Wait for engine\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine && window._gameEngine.characters && window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 20000 }\n  );\n  \n  // Open debug panel\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  \n  // Get all text content in the debug panel\n  const debugText = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    // Find the debug panel element\n    const allText = document.body.innerText;\n    return allText;\n  });\n  \n  console.log('=== PAGE TEXT ===');\n  console.log(debugText.substring(0, 3000));\n  \n  // Check what data properties exist on window\n  const engineInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    return {\n      properties: Object.keys(eng).filter(k => !k.startsWith('_') || k === '_gameEngine'),\n      characterKeys: eng.characters[0] ? Object.keys(eng.characters[0]) : [],\n      aiEngineKeys: eng.aiEngine ? Object.keys(eng.aiEngine) : [],\n      objectEngineKeys: eng.objectEngine ? Object.keys(eng.objectEngine) : [],\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== ENGINE INFO ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(engineInfo, null, 2));\n  \n  // Check characters more deeply  \n  const charDetails = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    return window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n      name: c.name,\n      label: c.label,\n      action: c.action,\n      targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n      currentActivity: c.currentActivity,\n      activityStartTime: c.activityStartTime,\n      activityDuration: c.activityDuration,\n    }));\n  });\n  console.log('=== CHARACTER DETAILS ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(charDetails, null, 2));\n  \n  // Check AI engine\n  const aiInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const ai = window._gameEngine.aiEngine;\n    if (!ai) return null;\n    return {\n      enabled: ai.enabled,\n      interval: ai.interval,\n      decisionLog: ai.decisionLog ? ai.decisionLog.slice(-5) : null,\n      eventLog: ai.eventLog ? ai.eventLog.slice(-5) : null,\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== AI ENGINE INFO ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(aiInfo, null, 2));\n  \n  await browser.close();\n})();\nEOF\nnode /tmp/probe-debug.js 2>&1",
  "timeout": 60000
}

    "input": {
      "command": "cat > /tmp/probe-debug.js << 'EOF'\n'use strict';\nconst { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');\nrequire('dotenv').config();\n\n(async () => {\n  const browser = await chromium.launch({ args: ['--enable-unsafe-swiftshader', '--disable-gpu'], headless: true });\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.goto(process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app');\n  \n  // Wait for engine\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => window._gameEngine && window._gameEngine.characters && window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    { timeout: 20000 }\n  );\n  \n  // Open debug panel\n  const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });\n  await debugBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  \n  // Get all text content in the debug panel\n  const debugText = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    // Find the debug panel element\n    const allText = document.body.innerText;\n    return allText;\n  });\n  \n  console.log('=== PAGE TEXT ===');\n  console.log(debugText.substring(0, 3000));\n  \n  // Check what data properties exist on window\n  const engineInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    return {\n      properties: Object.keys(eng).filter(k => !k.startsWith('_') || k === '_gameEngine'),\n      characterKeys: eng.characters[0] ? Object.keys(eng.characters[0]) : [],\n      aiEngineKeys: eng.aiEngine ? Object.keys(eng.aiEngine) : [],\n      objectEngineKeys: eng.objectEngine ? Object.keys(eng.objectEngine) : [],\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== ENGINE INFO ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(engineInfo, null, 2));\n  \n  // Check characters more deeply  \n  const charDetails = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    return window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n      name: c.name,\n      label: c.label,\n      action: c.action,\n      targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n      currentActivity: c.currentActivity,\n      activityStartTime: c.activityStartTime,\n      activityDuration: c.activityDuration,\n    }));\n  });\n  console.log('=== CHARACTER DETAILS ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(charDetails, null, 2));\n  \n  // Check AI engine\n  const aiInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const ai = window._gameEngine.aiEngine;\n    if (!ai) return null;\n    return {\n      enabled: ai.enabled,\n      interval: ai.interval,\n      decisionLog: ai.decisionLog ? ai.decisionLog.slice(-5) : null,\n      eventLog: ai.eventLog ? ai.eventLog.slice(-5) : null,\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== AI ENGINE INFO ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(aiInfo, null, 2));\n  \n  await browser.close();\n})();\nEOF\nnode /tmp/probe-debug.js 2>&1",
      "timeout": 60000
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

  "content": "'use strict';\n/**\n * PROBE \u2014 FRG-209\n * Temporary probe to understand debug panel structure and engine properties.\n * This test is NOT part of the final suite.\n */\nconst { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest('probe: debug panel text and engine structure', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await waitForEngine(page);\n\n  // Open debug panel\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n\n  const debugText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== PAGE TEXT (first 3000 chars) ===');\n  console.log(debugText.substring(0, 3000));\n\n  const engineInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    return {\n      engineKeys: Object.keys(eng),\n      characterKeys: eng.characters[0] ? Object.keys(eng.characters[0]) : [],\n      aiEngineKeys: eng.aiEngine ? Object.keys(eng.aiEngine) : [],\n      objectEngineKeys: eng.objectEngine ? Object.keys(eng.objectEngine) : [],\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== ENGINE KEYS ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(engineInfo, null, 2));\n\n  const charDetails = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    return window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n      name: c.name,\n      label: c.label,\n      action: c.action,\n      targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n      currentActivity: c.currentActivity,\n      activityStartTime: c.activityStartTime,\n      activityDuration: c.activityDuration,\n      walkTarget: c.walkTarget,\n    }));\n  });\n  console.log('=== CHARACTER DETAILS ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(charDetails, null, 2));\n\n  const aiInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const ai = window._gameEngine.aiEngine;\n    if (!ai) return 'no aiEngine';\n    return {\n      enabled: ai.enabled,\n      interval: ai.interval,\n      decisionLogLength: ai.decisionLog?.length ?? 'N/A',\n      decisionLogSample: ai.decisionLog?.slice(-3) ?? null,\n      eventLogLength: ai.eventLog?.length ?? 'N/A',\n      eventLogSample: ai.eventLog?.slice(-3) ?? null,\n      allKeys: Object.keys(ai),\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== AI ENGINE INFO ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(aiInfo, null, 2));\n\n  // wait for decisions and re-check\n  await page.waitForTimeout(10000);\n  const aiInfo2 = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const ai = window._gameEngine.aiEngine;\n    if (!ai) return 'no aiEngine';\n    return {\n      decisionLogLength: ai.decisionLog?.length ?? 'N/A',\n      decisionLogSample: ai.decisionLog?.slice(-3) ?? null,\n      eventLogLength: ai.eventLog?.length ?? 'N/A',\n      allKeys: Object.keys(ai),\n    };\n  });\n  console.log('=== AI ENGINE INFO (after 10s) ===');\n  console.log(JSON.stringify(aiInfo2, null, 2));\n\n  expect(true).toBe(true);\n});\n"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #54
Now I have a much clearer picture. Let me analyze: **Engine Structure:** - `_gameEngine.walkable` - the walkable grid - `_gameEngine.characters` - array of characters (Amelia/Maple, Lucy/Sol, Ash/Nyx) - `_gameEngine.objectEngine` - has `_map` property - `_gameEngine.aiEngine` - has `decisionIntervalMs`, `_timers`, `_pending`, `enabled` - **No decisionLog or eventLog** - these don't exist in the engine - `_gameEngine.simTimeMs`, `simSpeed` - Characters track their state through `action`, `targetObjectId`, `pendingAction`, `mood`, `energy`, `interactTimer`, `wanderTimer`, and `path` properties, but there's no `currentActivity` or `activityStartTime` tracking built in. The debug panel displays object states with individual "use" buttons and simulation time, but it's missing character state visibility and any decision logging—which aligns with the AI engine having no decision or event log infrastructure at all. This suggests the FRG-209 requirements around showing character states and decision logs in the debug interface aren't currently implemented. Now I'm checking the `decisionIntervalMs` configuration to confirm it's set to the expected 30-second interval.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #56
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "cat > /home/gem/project/tests/regression/probe-debug2.spec.js << 'EOF'\n'use strict';\nconst { test } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest('probe: AI interval and character interaction timer', async ({ page }) => {\n  test.setTimeout(90000);\n  const requests = [];\n  page.on('request', req => {\n    if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n      requests.push({ url: req.url(), ts: Date.now() });\n    }\n  });\n\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await waitForEngine(page);\n\n  const aiInterval = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs);\n  console.log('AI decisionIntervalMs:', aiInterval);\n\n  // Wait for 2 rounds of decisions (> 30s)\n  await page.waitForTimeout(40000);\n\n  // Check timings\n  console.log('Total LLM requests:', requests.length);\n  if (requests.length > 3) {\n    // Group by approximate 30s windows\n    const firstGroup = requests.filter(r => r.ts - requests[0].ts < 5000);\n    const secondGroup = requests.filter(r => r.ts - requests[0].ts >= 25000 && r.ts - requests[0].ts <= 45000);\n    console.log('First batch (0-5s):', firstGroup.length);\n    console.log('Second batch (25-45s):', secondGroup.length);\n  }\n\n  // Check interaction timer\n  const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n      label: c.label,\n      action: c.action,\n      interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n      interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n      targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n      pendingAction: c.pendingAction,\n    }))\n  );\n  console.log('Character states after 40s:', JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));\n\n  // Check all action types seen\n  const allActions = new Set();\n  for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n    const s = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => c.action));\n    s.forEach(a => allActions.add(a));\n    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  }\n  console.log('Action types observed:', [...allActions]);\n\n  // Check what \"Full dashboard\" link leads to\n  const dashLink = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const links = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a'));\n    return links.map(l => ({ text: l.textContent, href: l.href }));\n  });\n  console.log('All links:', JSON.stringify(dashLink, null, 2));\n});\nEOF\nnpx playwright test tests/regression/probe-debug2.spec.js --reporter=list 2>&1",
  "timeout": 180000
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01VgcLRWgWEg8w9DpHhk3Gho",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "cat > /home/gem/project/tests/regression/probe-debug2.spec.js << 'EOF'\n'use strict';\nconst { test } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n\ntest('probe: AI interval and character interaction timer', async ({ page }) => {\n  test.setTimeout(90000);\n  const requests = [];\n  page.on('request', req => {\n    if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n      requests.push({ url: req.url(), ts: Date.now() });\n    }\n  });\n\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await waitForEngine(page);\n\n  const aiInterval = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs);\n  console.log('AI decisionIntervalMs:', aiInterval);\n\n  // Wait for 2 rounds of decisions (> 30s)\n  await page.waitForTimeout(40000);\n\n  // Check timings\n  console.log('Total LLM requests:', requests.length);\n  if (requests.length > 3) {\n    // Group by approximate 30s windows\n    const firstGroup = requests.filter(r => r.ts - requests[0].ts < 5000);\n    const secondGroup = requests.filter(r => r.ts - requests[0].ts >= 25000 && r.ts - requests[0].ts <= 45000);\n    console.log('First batch (0-5s):', firstGroup.length);\n    console.log('Second batch (25-45s):', secondGroup.length);\n  }\n\n  // Check interaction timer\n  const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n      label: c.label,\n      action: c.action,\n      interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n      interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n      targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n      pendingAction: c.pendingAction,\n    }))\n  );\n  console.log('Character states after 40s:', JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));\n\n  // Check all action types seen\n  const allActions = new Set();\n  for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n    const s = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => c.action));\n    s.forEach(a => allActions.add(a));\n    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n  }\n  console.log('Action types observed:', [...allActions]);\n\n  // Check what \"Full dashboard\" link leads to\n  const dashLink = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const links = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a'));\n    return links.map(l => ({ text: l.textContent, href: l.href }));\n  });\n  console.log('All links:', JSON.stringify(dashLink, null, 2));\n});\nEOF\nnpx playwright test tests/regression/probe-debug2.spec.js --reporter=list 2>&1",
      "timeout": 180000
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #58
Excellent! This is very informative: 1. `decisionIntervalMs: 30000` - confirmed 30s cycle 2. Total LLM requests: only 3 in 40s - this means the second batch (after 30s) didn't fire. Interesting! This might be because: - All characters are `interacting` (they don't get a new decision while interacting) - The 30s timer only fires a new decision when characters are ready 3. Character states after 40s - all `interacting`: - Maple: interactTimer=17725ms of interactTimerMax=30000ms (stove - 30s duration!) - Sol: interactTimer=10519ms of interactTimerMax=35000ms - Nyx: interactTimer=21650ms of interactTimerMax=45000ms 4. Action types seen: only `interacting` (all three were interacting the whole time) 5. Debug panel has a "↗ Full dashboard" link to `/debug.html` 6. Ah, I see now - the issue specifically mentions testing against `/debug.html`, not the main page, which is marked as M2. The existing tests all run against the root path and access the debug panel through a button, so "debug page" might refer to that debug view. But there's also a dedicated `/debug.html` endpoint I should check out. I've confirmed the 30-second decision cycle, character interaction states, and interaction timer durations, but noticed the repeat cycle doesn't trigger while characters are busy—possibly intentional. The app is missing decision and event logs in the engine, and the debug panel doesn't expose character states. Let me explore what `/debug.html` offers as a full dashboard.

Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "cat > /home/gem/project/tests/regression/probe-debug3.spec.js << 'EOF'\n'use strict';\nconst { test } = require('@playwright/test');\n\ntest('probe: /debug.html full dashboard structure', async ({ page }) => {\n  test.setTimeout(60000);\n\n  await page.goto('/debug.html');\n  await page.waitForTimeout(5000);\n\n  const title = await page.title();\n  console.log('Debug page title:', title);\n\n  const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);\n  console.log('=== DEBUG PAGE TEXT ===');\n  console.log(bodyText.substring(0, 5000));\n\n  // Check if the game engine is present\n  const hasEngine = await page.evaluate(() => !!window._gameEngine);\n  console.log('Has _gameEngine:', hasEngine);\n\n  // Check URL, headings, etc.\n  const headings = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h1, h2, h3, h4')).map(h => h.textContent.trim())\n  );\n  console.log('Headings:', headings);\n\n  const sections = await page.evaluate(() =>\n    Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id]')).map(el => ({ id: el.id, tag: el.tagName, text: el.textContent.substring(0, 50) }))\n  );\n  console.log('Sections with IDs:', JSON.stringify(sections, null, 2));\n});\nEOF\nnpx playwright test tests/regression/probe-debug3.spec.js --reporter=list 2>&1",
  "timeout": 90000
}
